subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, World Bank, IDB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Bolivian official (INE, BCB) + Bolivian/Spanish-language press (read partly via Anglophone aggregators).

A state-led economic model in crisis: a gas-export collapse, depleted reserves, a USD peg under severe strain, surging inflation, and a 2025 election that ended ~20 years of one-party rule amid deep crisis.

Standout drags: a fiscal/currency/gas crisis, weak rule of law, and surging inflation; a possible reform reset and low homicide are offsets. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone; Spanish primary press under-read.

economic.notes: A state-led model in acute crisis - a gas-export collapse, depleted reserves, a crumbling peg, and surging inflation.

fiscal_state.notes: A deficit ~13% GDP, debt ~95%, reserves under $2B (mostly gold), and a gas-revenue collapse with a fuel-subsidy/import crisis. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: A USD peg under severe strain - a dollar shortage and a wide parallel-market gap, with deficits monetised - forced adjustment likely. (IMF; CEIC)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The boliviano has no international role. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Surged from ~2% to ~25% after years of stability. (INE; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Dollar-shortage-stressed, with fragile deposit confidence and no FX backstop. (IMF; State Dept ICS)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Collapsing gas exports (underinvestment), huge but barely-developed lithium reserves, and a mining/soy economy in crisis - the first GDP contraction in 40 years. (IMF; INE)

institutional.notes: A weak, politicised rule of law, with usable statistics and a pressured-but-free press.

rule_of_law.notes: A low WJP standing, a politicised/elected judiciary, prior lawfare, and corruption. (WJP 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Usable and publishing (the recession confirmed), with some politicisation. (INE; IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and patronage-laden, hollowed by the fiscal crisis. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Relatively free but pressured, with judicial harassment and state-advertising leverage. (RSF 2025/2026)

political_social.notes: A model-collapse political crisis with a possible reform reset, against deep regional cleavages.

political_stability.notes: The state-led model collapsed amid a bitter intra-party split, fuel/dollar protests, and a 2024 alleged coup attempt; a 2025 election ended ~20 years of one-party rule amid deep crisis - turmoil now, a possible reform reset later. (AS/COA; NBC)

civil_liberties.notes: Formal freedoms intact but protest-heavy, with recurrent blockades and crackdowns. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited; not a relocation destination, with thin infrastructure for migrants. (IOM)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep highland-indigenous-versus-lowland and party-versus-opposition cleavages, with high inequality. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: A young population and a favourable age structure - a relative positive. (INE; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A foreign-policy realignment in flux, with internal unrest.

alliance_reliability.notes: Formerly a Russia/China/Venezuela-aligned bloc member; a 2025 election may shift it Western-ward - in flux. (AS/COA)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external war, with internal unrest/protest and a landlocked-dispute legacy. (ACLED)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: FX/capital controls amid the dollar crisis; not sanctioned. (IMF; State Dept ICS)

physical_practical.notes: Andean water stress and Amazon wildfires, against a relatively low homicide rate.

climate.notes: Andean glacial-melt water stress (the capital's supply) and record 2024 Amazon wildfires, with flood/drought. (IAEA; Copernicus)

healthcare.notes: Weak and uneven, with crisis-driven drug/import shortages. (WHO)

crime_safety.notes: Homicide low for the region (~3/100k) - a relative bright spot - but with rising trafficking violence and crisis-driven property crime. (police data; InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against flood/wildfire/water exposure. (World Bank)

summary: A state-led economic model in acute crisis: collapsing gas exports, reserves under $2 billion, a USD peg under severe strain with a dollar shortage and parallel-market gap, deficits around 13% of GDP, and inflation surged to ~25% after years of stability, with the first GDP contraction in 40 years. Politics convulsed through a model collapse, an intra-party split, fuel and dollar protests, and a 2025 election that ended roughly two decades of one-party rule amid deep crisis. Weak rule of law, Andean water stress, and Amazon wildfires weigh further; a possible reform reset, a young population, and an unusually low homicide rate are the offsets.
